body.title: Base shim: 4 panes + common skill verbs for all devices
body.result: BaseShim: handle_command + 5 common skills + _tokenize; ScrapsShim barks; NannyShim schedule voice; 18 tests (379ef63e)
body.description:
BaseShim provides 4 panes (status, chat, controls, feed) to every device regardless of whether it has an LLM. Non-LLM devices get a stub chat handler — Scraps barks, Nanny talks about her tasks, tokenizing shim returns numeric array of token ids (handy for unit testing).

Every device has skills. Common base: /help (list skills), /health (same as health web page, text format), /stop, /resume, /feed. Device-specific skills extend from base. Design rule: common verbs and nouns first — don't invent per-device names for shared concepts.

The tokenizing stub: any non-skill input to a non-LLM device gets tokenized and the token id array returned as the response. Funny but useful — unit tests can verify tokenization without needing LLM.

**Affected files:** unseen_university/skeleton/skeleton.py (base skill protocol), devices/*/shim.py (per-device stubs), docs/TheoryOfOperation.md (skill section)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; OOP-first (skill registry in BaseShim); device-specific skills override but do not replace common ones
**Scope boundary:** IN: common skill set (/help /health /stop /resume /feed) in BaseShim; stub chat/tokenize handlers for non-LLM devices; Scraps + Nanny as reference implementations. OUT: LLM skill routing, web pane rendering
**Completion criteria:** import BaseShim; attach stub to Scraps and Nanny; /help returns common skills; /health returns text health; non-skill input to Scraps returns barks; non-skill input to tokenizing device returns token int array; tests cover all
